binding: Scenario B (count-based n_eff), HoI-ratified 13 Jul 2026. ANCHORS are the canonical 2007 Royal Canadian Mint proof set (fixture PCS-CA-2026-0007), the v01→v02 evidence ladder — Silver when custody is thin, Gold once provenance is supplied — reproduced to the digit at the coins scaleFactor (10). They prove the deterministic engine on a known coin. The scorer's ability to FLAG a fake is proven category-independently by the §12 golden (fake Daytona → Flagged) and by the material red-flag mapping. SOFT-LAUNCH STANCE: the honesty ceiling disclaims authentication and the tier maps on the lower bound, so a category is calibrated on genuine, real-object field_golden entries; a known-fake entry is recommended and added when one is sourced, not a launch blocker. FIELD_GOLDEN are real objects run end-to-end through coins.v1.json → enrich → score, each with an expert-assigned tier and a named ground-truth source.

field_golden.name: 2007 Royal Canadian Mint proof set
field_golden.expectedTierNote: Silver as documented (custody thin in the reference evidence). Full provenance — sealed COA + purchase receipt — lifts it to Gold; the v01→v02 anchor ladder proves that rung. A genuine set is never Flagged.
field_golden.groundTruthSource: Genuine owner-held RCM proof set; documentary record (RCM 2007 issue) corroborated by PCGS + Numista Tier-1.
